steps.title: Find the constant ratio
steps.content: Since the ratio of $k$ to $p$ is constant, we have $$\frac{k}{p} = \text{constant}.$$ Using the given values $k=5$ and $p=4$, $$\frac{k}{p} = \frac{5}{4}.$$

steps.title: Compute k for p=28
steps.content: For $p=28$, multiply the constant ratio by $p$: $$k = \frac{5}{4} \times 28 = 5 \times 7 = 35.$$ The answer is 35.
